terminal_info: Barcelona El Prat Airport has two active passenger terminals: Terminal 1 (T1) and Terminal 2 (T2). Car rental counters are located in the arrivals area of both terminals. Terminal 1 is the main international terminal and houses the majority of major rental suppliers. Terminal 2 is used primarily by low-cost carriers. Confirm which terminal your flight arrives at and proceed to the arrivals level for rental counters.
meta_title: Car Rental at BCN — Barcelona El Prat Airport
meta_description: Find car rental options at Barcelona El Prat Airport (BCN). Pickup info, terminal details, and tips for renting a car at BCN.
h1: Car Rental at Barcelona El Prat Airport (BCN)
intro_paragraph: Barcelona El Prat Airport is the main international gateway to Catalonia and one of the busiest airports in Spain, handling a large volume of European and intercontinental traffic year-round. Car rental counters are located on the arrivals level of both terminals, making the pickup process convenient for arriving passengers. The airport sits approximately 14 kilometres southwest of Barcelona city center, with good motorway access via the C-31 and the Gran Via. From the airport, rental car drivers heading into the city or toward the wider Catalan region have straightforward access to the motorway network. The Costa Brava, Tarragona, and the Pyrenees are all reachable within two hours from the airport.

faq.question: Where are the rental car counters at Barcelona Airport?
faq.answer: Rental car counters at Barcelona El Prat Airport are located in the arrivals area of both Terminal 1 and Terminal 2. In Terminal 1, counters are on the arrivals level and clearly signposted. In Terminal 2, counters are in a similar position in the arrivals hall. Vehicle collection is typically from a covered parking area adjacent to the terminal building. Follow signs for car rental upon exiting the baggage reclaim area.

faq.question: How far is Barcelona Airport from the city center?
faq.answer: Barcelona El Prat Airport is approximately 14 kilometres southwest of Barcelona city center. By car on the C-31 motorway or the Gran Via, the journey typically takes between 20 and 40 minutes, though this can extend during peak traffic periods, particularly on weekday mornings and Friday evenings.

faq.question: Is it easy to drive from Barcelona Airport to other parts of Catalonia?
faq.answer: Yes. The airport has direct access to the Catalan motorway network, making it straightforward to head in any direction from the airport without driving through the city. The AP-7 motorway connects north toward the Costa Brava and France, and south toward Tarragona and Valencia. The C-32 provides access to the Garraf coast. The main roads are clearly signposted in both Catalan and Spanish.
